Feedback_Loops.Mechanism: An initial disruption causes Dysbiosis, which increases Leaky Gut. Leaky Gut allows pathogens to enter the bloodstream, which increases the activation of the Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Adrenal Axis. This neuroendocrine stress response crosses domains via the Gut-Brain Axis and increases Sickness Behavior (depression and anxiety). The psychological stress from Sickness Behavior then feeds back into the biological domain, where it increases further Dysbiosis, creating a self-amplifying cascade of declining health.

The introduction of a Psychobiotic increases the health and diversity of the Microbiota. A healthy Microbiota catalyzes Fermentation of dietary fibers, which increases the production of Short-Chain Fatty Acids. These Short-Chain Fatty Acids act to heal the intestinal lining, which inhibits Leaky Gut, and they also inhibit the hyperactive stress response of the Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Adrenal Axis. This systemic calming decreases Sickness Behavior, returning the Hologenome to a state of balance.

When extreme Dysbiosis drives severe Leaky Gut and Sickness Behavior, medical interventions such as a Fecal Microbial Transplant or Phage Therapy are deployed. These interventions inhibit Dysbiosis directly by eradicating pathogens and repopulating the gut with a healthy Microbiota. As Dysbiosis decreases, the subsequent inflammatory triggers are removed, which decreases Leaky Gut, dampens the Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Adrenal Axis, and ultimately decreases Sickness Behavior.
